title: Polyspora elliptica
intro: Polyspora elliptica, also known as the Polyspora tree, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Theaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Polyspora elliptica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Polyspora elliptica is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, tonics, and ointments to harness its therapeutic benefits.
